overview: Lurrus Breach is a U/B/R Vintage tempo deck that leverages graveyard recursion and instant-speed card advantage to overwhelm opponents. By repeatedly casting Psychic Frog and copying its effects with Flow State, the deck generates massive card draw while applying relentless pressure. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as the central engine, enabling efficient reanimation and hand refills to outmaneuver traditional control and combo strategies in the Vintage environment.
win_conditions: Repeatedly attacking with Psychic Frog to draw into answers and close out games
Lurrus recursion loops to bypass opponent's removal and counterspells
Flow State card advantage to stabilize and out-resource control decks

keep_criteria: Hands containing Lurrus of the Dream-Den or Psychic Frog
Two-land hands with early interaction or Flow State
Hands with a clear path to turn-one or turn-two recursion
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetch/shock or duals), at least one Lurrus, and a Flow State or Psychic Frog.
Keep hands with Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, or Lightning Strike for early disruption.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or recursion engines
Hands heavy on late-game cards without early setup
Hands vulnerable to common Vintage disruption without answers
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only high-CMC spells, or hands missing Lurrus/Flow State/Psychic Frog without disruption or mana fixing.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and playing Lurrus or a cheap threat
Use Flow State on opponent's turn to draw into early interaction
Begin filling the graveyard with creatures for Lurrus activation
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize targeting key interaction or combo pieces.
Turn 2: Play a shockland/dual, cast a cheap cantrip or disruption, or drop Lurrus if you have the mana.
Prioritize fetching/finding Flow State and Psychic Frog while cycling through the deck with Lurrus-recursable cantrips.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast Flow State on turn 3-4.
mid_game: Activate Lurrus to return Psychic Frog or other threats to hand
Cast Flow State to copy Frog's attack trigger or draw multiple cards
Apply pressure while holding up mana for counterspells or removal
Balance graveyard resources for Lurrus recursion against keeping cards in hand for immediate disruption.
Avoid over-committing to casting cheap spells if you need to hold mana for counters or removal.
Use cantrips to dig for Flow State/Psychic Frog while maintaining tempo and hand size.
Commit threats only when you have protection or can answer their interaction.
Hold back on casting Flow State if you lack sufficient targets or if the opponent has sweepers.
Use Lurrus to recur removal/cantrips to maintain board presence without depleting your hand.
late_game: Loop Lurrus and Frog to generate insurmountable card advantage
Close out games by attacking with Frog repeatedly to draw into finishers
Stabilize against board wipes by keeping threats in hand via Lurrus
Flow State + Psychic Frog (often with a Frog token or other cheap spells to hit the mana value threshold).
Lurrus recursion of Psychic Frog or other win-cons to close out the game.
Go all-in when you have Flow State, Psychic Frog, and enough cheap spells to hit lethal.
Commit when the opponent has no interaction left or when you can race their combo before they stabilize.

key_cards.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
key_cards.notes: Activate to return creatures from graveyard to hand, enabling repeated casting and bypassing removal

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast on opponent's turn to draw cards, or use to copy Psychic Frog's attack trigger for massive draws

key_cards.name: Psychic Frog
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and card engine
key_cards.notes: Attack to draw cards, then return to hand with Lurrus to repeat the process indefinitely

sequencing_tips: Hold Flow State until opponent's turn to maximize card draw and find answers
Activate Lurrus before attacking with Frog to ensure you can recast it next turn
Prioritize keeping Frog in hand over graveyard when opponent has board wipes
Use Lurrus to return threats to hand rather than graveyard to avoid hate cards
Common mistake: Over-casting cheap spells early and running out of resources for the combo.
Always keep a hand of disruption and a win-con.
Unique interaction: Psychic Frog can be cast for {1}{U}{R} and creates a Frog token, which can then be used to cast another Psychic Frog for free, or combined with Flow State for massive value.
Lurrus recursion timing: Don't cast spells you can't afford to lose to the graveyard unless you have a way to recur them.
Format note: Since format is Unknown, assume standard rules but adapt land counts and hate cards based on actual format legality and local metagame shifts.

matchups.advice: Pressure early with Frog attacks and use Flow State to draw into their key spells before they stabilize
Use Flow State for card advantage to outvalue them and hold up removal for their key threats
Disrupt their setup with instant-speed interaction and race them with Lurrus recursion loops

Use cheap, uncounterable threats (Psychic Frog can be cast for free if you control a Frog, or use Flow State to bypass counters). Sequence cantrips to find protection. Use Lurrus to recur spells after counters resolve, and pressure with discard to remove counters from hand.

Apply early pressure with discard and removal. Don't let them stabilize. Use Blood Moon or discard to disrupt mana bases. Combo off quickly before they cast their big spells or establish board dominance.

Win the disruption race. Prioritize Thoughtseize and discard to remove their Lurrus/Flow State/Psychic Frog. Use Lurrus to recur removal/cantrips. Track graveyard for recursion targets. Win by going first or having better sequencing and hand management.

common_threats: Counterspells that stop Flow State or Frog
Board wipes that clear Lurrus and threats
Direct removal targeting Lurrus or Frog
Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Anger of the Gods), counterspells, graveyard hate, and combo pieces.
Note when opponents tap out for interaction versus when they're holding priority.
Track graveyard contents for Lurrus recursion targets and monitor opponent's hand size to gauge combo readiness.
